title: 10 best herbal creams for pink eye

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with pink eye because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb calendula, also known as pot marigold, has been traditionally used to treat various eye issues, including conjunctivitis or pink eye. The cream's antimicrobial properties also help combat bacterial or viral infections that may be causing the condition. By applying a thin layer of pot marigold cream to affected eyes, you can quickly alleviate discomfort and promote natural healing.
remedy_properties: Calendula's anti-inflammatory property: Calendula helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, which alleviates discomfort and redness associated with pink eye.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Calendula's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate pink eye symptoms, while its antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial or viral infections causing the condition.

Soften and lubricate the cornea property: Calendula's emollient and soothing properties help soften and lubricate the affected area, promoting healing and reducing irritation by creating a protective barrier against further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Seeds: Contain antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial infections causing conjunctivitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers in full bloom, collecting at least one cup of fresh petals for the recipe.
Dry the collected calendula petals using a dehydrator on low heat for 2 hours or air drying for 3-4 days.
Combine one tablespoon of dried calendula petals with two tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar, leaving room at top for mixing.
Infuse the mixture by placing the jar in a warm water bath at 160°f to 180°f for 30 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding solids.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax and one tablespoon of vitamin e oil to the infused oil mixture, heating gently until the wax is fully melted and the cream has thickened within 10-15 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Purple coneflower contains compounds like triterpenoids and sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to combat infections and reduce inflammation in pink eye.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's active ingredients, such as apigenin and luteolin, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye.

Immune-boosting properties: Purple coneflower has been traditionally used to support the immune system, and its compounds may help stimulate the body's natural defense against infections, such as pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain a high concentration of phenolic compounds, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pink eye symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the purple coneflower are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, making them an excellent source for soothing and calming the irritated tissues associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea using air circulation or low-temperature dehydration equipment for 3-5 days.
Combine 10 grams of dried echinacea purpurea with 50 grams of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at a temperature below 150°f for 30 minutes to extract the active compounds from the echinacea, then strain through cheesecloth.
Mix the resulting infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil, melting at low heat until smooth and creamy texture is achieved.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with pink eye because they contain the antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of echinacea, a natural ingredient that has been used for centuries to treat various eye infections. The cream's active compounds, such as cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid, work together to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing in the affected eye. This natural approach can provide fast and effective relief from pink eye symptoms, helping to clear up discharge, itching, and redness quickly.

Aloe creams helps with pink eye because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The gel-like substance in aloe contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling, alleviating discomfort and itchiness associated with pink eye. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of aloe help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause pink eye, promoting healing and recovery. As a result, applying herbal aloe creams to the affected area helps to alleviate symptoms and promote natural healing.
remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids helps to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye, allowing for faster recovery.

Antimicrobial and antiviral: The anthraquinones present in aloe vera have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause pink eye, promoting the healing process and preventing further infection.

Wound-healing and regenerative: Aloe vera's polysaccharides help to stimulate collagen production, promote tissue repair, and enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms, allowing for faster recovery and reduced scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in vitamins and minerals, aloe roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe redness and discomfort associated with pink eye.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain aloin, a natural compound that has antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against bacterial and fungal infections that cause pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Gather aloe vera gel from a mature aloe plant extract and measure 2 tablespoons into a clean glass bowl.
Combine aloe vera gel with 1 tablespoon of calendula flowers and 1 tablespoon of chamomile flowers in the bowl.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse with the aloe vera.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bowl to remove the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and stir well to combine the herbal extract with the coconut oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to reduce swelling and redness associated with conjunctivitis. Additionally, licorice root has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, which can exacerbate pink eye symptoms. By applying a topical herbal licorice cream to the affected area, it can provide relief from itchiness, burning, and discharge, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal licorice cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as glycyrrhizin and liquiritin, which help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Licorice root has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activities against various microorganisms, including those that can cause conjunctivitis (pink eye), helping to combat the infection.

Soften and lubricate the eyes: The demulcent properties of licorice cream help soothe and moisturize the affected area, reducing dryness, itchiness, and discomfort, making it an effective topical treatment for pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing redness and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant contain triterpenoid saponins, which have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, helping to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots in summer season weighing approximately 100 grams.
Dry the harvested roots under shade for three weeks or until they become brittle and lightweight.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle taking about 30 minutes to complete.
Mix one part of the powdered root with two parts of coconut oil and one part of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Stir the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a temperature of 70°c allowing it to cool before filling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with pink eye because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and reduce redness and swelling in the affected area. The cream's natural ingredients, such as dandelion root and leaves, also have antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial infections, promoting healing and preventing further infection. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, allowing the eye to recover quickly and naturally.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pink eye.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in dandelion, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, help combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye, such as Haemophilus influenzae and Streptococcus pneumoniae.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds, like vitamins A, C, and E, neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the progression of pink eye and its associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots are often used to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Fruits: High in vitamins and minerals, the fruits provide a gentle and nourishing base for medicinal creams, helping to promote healthy eye function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves, weighing out approximately 2 ounces for the recipe preparation.
Combine the harvested plant material with 4 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure efficient extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler, melting the mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with pink eye because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's active ingredients, such as tinctures of eyebright herbs, work to reduce swelling and inflammation, thereby alleviating the discomfort and redness associated with conjunctivitis. Additionally, eyebright creams help to promote the healing process by providing a protective barrier that shields the eye from irritants and bacteria, allowing the body's natural defenses to take over and recover from the infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eyebright creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antimicrobial agents: Herbal eyebright creams often contain antimicrobial agents like essential oils (e.g., tea tree oil) or herbs (e.g., calendula), which help kill bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause pink eye, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Soothing and protective properties: Eyebright creams often include soothing and protective ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, or vitamin E, which help calm irritated eyes, reduce discomfort, and protect the delicate skin around the eyes from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion associated with pink eye.
Barks: Eyebright barks are rich in flavonoids, which have antibacterial properties that help to combat bacterial infections causing pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning or evening.
Dry the collected material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind dried euphrasia officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 20% euphrasia officinalis powder with 80% petroleum jelly to create the cream base within 15 minutes.
Store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with pink eye because they contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's active ingredients, such as plantain leaf extract, work to reduce redness and swelling, while also combating the underlying bacterial infection that causes pink eye. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Chop the washed plantago major leaves into small pieces and add 100 grams of dried chamomile flowers to create a mixture.
Combine the chopped plantago major leaves and dried chamomile flowers with 200 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 24 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids, and add 10 grams of beeswax to create a cream base, which takes about 30 minutes to melt at low heat.
Pour the cooled cream base into sterilized tubes or containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months or until used.

Dry the plant material using air circulation or machine drying for at least 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried symphytum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix the herbal powder with 50g of beeswax and 25g of coconut oil in a double boiler heating gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard solids then pour into sterilized jars and let cool completely.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 4 hours.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 2 cups of boiling water to steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth and combine it with 8 ounces of cooled petroleum jelly or coconut oil.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens into a creamy consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers at dawn in june and july for maximum potency.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm air current for 24 hours or using a food dehydrator for 12 hours.
Combine dried flowers with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler, heating to 180°f for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids after 30 minutes of settling time.
Store the cream in an airtight jar in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks and apply topically three times daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried sanguisorba minor leaves and stems.
Grind 1 ounce of the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 8 ounces of distilled water in a glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a warm infusion.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and allowing the cream to cool.

Combine the ground vaccinium myrtillus berries with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and leaving behind the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax in a double boiler, melting the waxes at 70°c for 15 minutes.
Pour the melted wax mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

Combine the powdered achyrocline satureioides with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth to remove any solids and allow it to cool to room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried marrubium vulgare flowers and 20 grams of beeswax.
Combine the dried flowers with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to infuse the flowers' properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and then dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 6 hours.
Steep 100g of dried valerian root in 500ml of boiling water for 20 minutes to create an infusion.

Combine 20 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora flowers, 10 grams of dried chamomile flowers, and 5 grams of beeswax pellets with 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a warm water bath at 60°c for 30 minutes to create a smooth and consistent texture.
Pour the hot herbal cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to six months or use within three weeks.

Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye conditions like conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with pink eye.
Barks: Rosemary bark has been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties, which can help combat the bacterial infections that often cause pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine the ground rosmarinus officinalis powder with 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl and simmer for 15 minutes at 75°c to 85°c.
Strain the resulting liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 40g of coconut oil and 30ml of the rosmarinus officinalis infusion in a double boiler at 65°c to 75°c for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken at room temperature for 60 minutes before transferring it to glass jars for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for pink eye are natural remedies that soothe and calm irritated eyes caused by bacterial or viral infections. These creams contain ingredients such as aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Examples of herbal creams that effectively treat pink eye include those containing echinacea, calendula, and goldenseal. By using these creams, people with pink eye can experience improved vision, reduced redness and swelling, and faster recovery times, ultimately improving their quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat pink eye with herbal creams is by applying a soothing and antibacterial mixture of ingredients. A combination of calendula oil, tea tree oil, and aloe vera gel has proven effective in reducing inflammation and preventing infection. Apply a thin layer of the cream to the affected area several times a day to promote healing and relief from discomfort. This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms without harsh chemicals or side effects.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pink eye that are treated with herbal creams are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and styes. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the affected areas. These creams can reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with these conditions, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.
